name: Stytch MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Stytch MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Stytch's authentication platform — user management, session management, magic link operations, passkey configuration, and organization management.
use_cases: Managing Stytch users and user profiles from agent workflows
Checking session validity and user authentication state
Managing organization memberships in B2B apps via agents
Auditing authentication events and user activity
not_for: Consumer-facing auth flows (this is the management API)
best_when: An agent needs to manage users, sessions, or authentication configurations in a Stytch-backed application.
avoid_when: You're using Auth0, Clerk, or WorkOS — use those providers' MCPs instead.

auth.notes: Project ID + Secret key pair (STYTCH_PROJECT_ID + STYTCH_SECRET). Separate test and live credentials. No scope granularity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 25,000 MAU, core authentication
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for small apps. B2B features on Growth plan. MCP is open source.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits apply; not prominently documented

known_agent_gotchas: Test vs live environments require different project ID/secret pairs
B2B vs Consumer APIs are different — ensure correct API endpoint used
Session tokens are short-lived — agents must handle session refresh
Rate limits not prominently documented — add conservative backoff
API credentials lack scope granularity — full account access
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Auth-provider grade security. SOC 2 Type II, GDPR. Test/live environment separation is good security practice.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Authentication provider with strong security posture. SOC 2 Type II. Scope granularity gap on credentials.
